title: Helia nerioides
intro: Helianthus neriifolius, commonly known as Helia nerioides, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve heart health, and protect against cancer. The bioactive constituents of Helia nerioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Helia nerioides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into capsules and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Helia nerioides is classified under the family Acanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thistle family. This classification places Helia nerioides within the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The family Acanthaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Helia nerioides falls within one of these subfamilies. The exact classification of Helia nerioides may vary depending on the source and any recent revisions to the plant's taxonomy. Further research may be necessary to determine the most up-to-date classification for this plant.
